description: Free single-page web tool that returns Kua number, East/West group, and eight personal directions with plain-English meanings. Flask + vanilla JS. Self-contained methodology page (chapter 6 copy). Optional couples mode. Embeddable.

userStories.title: Flask skeleton and project files
userStories.description: Stand up the Flask app shell so subsequent tasks have a working substrate. Routes for /, /methodology, /embed wired with empty templates. Static asset folders created. Deps locked.
acceptanceCriteria: app/app.py exists and Flask app instance is named 'app'.
Routes registered for '/', '/methodology', '/embed' (templates may be stubs at this stage).
templates/base.html defines a single layout with title block, meta block, and content block.
static/css/main.css and static/css/print.css exist (may be empty placeholders).
static/js/kua.js, directions.js, ui.js exist (may be empty placeholders).
pyproject.toml declares flask and markdown as deps via uv-compatible PEP 621 format.
README.md documents `uv run flask --app app.app run` as the dev command.
.gitignore excludes .venv/, __pycache__/, *.pyc, .env.
userStories.notes: All routes and template/static stubs in place. /embed sets frame-ancestors '*' from the route handler so it can ship before EMBED-001 polishes the slim layout.

userStories.title: Copy chapter 6 into content/methodology.md and render at /methodology
userStories.description: Copy projects/feng-shui/content/06-compass-school.md verbatim into projects/kua-calculator/content/methodology.md. Render it as HTML via the markdown package at /methodology. Inline SVGs from the source must render as-is (markdown package must allow raw HTML).
acceptanceCriteria: content/methodology.md exists and matches chapter 6 verbatim.
GET /methodology returns 200 and renders the markdown as HTML.
Inline SVG diagrams from the source render visibly.
Headings render with anchor-friendly IDs so deep links from the result page work (e.g. #5-the-kua-number).
Page uses the base template (header, footer, brand styles).
userStories.notes: Python-markdown extras+toc gives heading IDs that match the anchors SEO-001 will deep-link to (#5-the-kua-number, #6-the-eight-mansions-ba-zhai, #east-group-and-west-group). md_in_html (from 'extra') lets the inline SVG figures pass through unchanged.

userStories.title: Pure client-side Kua calculation module
userStories.description: Implement static/js/kua.js with the calculation logic. Exposes a single function `calculateKua(year, gender)` that returns an integer 1..9 with the Kua 5 reassignment rules handled. Includes the post-2000 formula variant. Pure function, no DOM dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: Function `calculateKua(year, gender)` exported on window (or as ES module).
Male 1978 returns 4.
Female 1985 returns 9.
Male born when reduction yields 5 returns 2 (Kua 5 male reassignment).
Female born when sum yields 5 returns 8 (Kua 5 female reassignment).
Post-2000 male uses (9 - reduced digit) instead of (10 - reduced digit).
Post-2000 female uses (reduced digit + 6) instead of (reduced digit + 5).
Function validates year is a 4-digit integer >= 1900 and <= current year + 1, throws TypeError otherwise.
Function returns East-or-West group as a second exported helper `kuaGroup(kua)` returning 'east' or 'west'.
userStories.notes: Verified manually against chapter 6 examples: male 1978 -> 4, female 1985 -> 9, male 1995 -> 5 then reassigned to 2, female 1990 -> 5 then reassigned to 8. Post-2000 variants tested with year 2003 (male->6, female->9).

userStories.title: 8-Kua direction-quality lookup table with plain-English meanings
userStories.description: Implement static/js/directions.js with the data structure mapping every Kua number to its 8 directional qualities (Sheng Qi, Tian Yi, Yan Nian, Fu Wei, Huo Hai, Wu Gui, Liu Sha, Jue Ming). Each direction includes the compass label, the pinyin name, the English gloss, and a one-line plain-English meaning. Honest framing throughout.
acceptanceCriteria: Export `directionsForKua(kua)` returning an object with keys for all 8 compass directions (N, NE, E, SE, S, SW, W, NW).
Each direction object includes: { quality, pinyin, gloss, meaning, favourable }.
All four favourable qualities (Sheng Qi, Tian Yi, Yan Nian, Fu Wei) and four unfavourable (Huo Hai, Wu Gui, Liu Sha, Jue Ming) appear for every Kua.
Plain-English meanings use 'supports the conditions for' language, never 'will' or 'guarantees'.
Kua 4 male example from chapter 6 verifies: SE=Sheng Qi, E=Tian Yi, S=Yan Nian, N=Fu Wei, SW=Huo Hai, NE=Wu Gui, NW=Liu Sha, W=Jue Ming.
Kua 9 example from chapter 6 verifies: E=Sheng Qi, SE=Tian Yi, N=Yan Nian, S=Fu Wei, W=Huo Hai, NW=Wu Gui, SW=Liu Sha, NE=Jue Ming.
userStories.notes: Kua 4 and 9 mappings verified position-by-position against chapter 6. The other six Kuas use the cultural-commons Eight Mansions table, preserving the East/West group structure (East-group favourable always lands in {N,S,E,SE}; West-group favourable always lands in {NE,SW,W,NW}). Also added orderedDirectionsForKua so UI-001 can render favourable-first without re-sorting.

userStories.title: Input form and single-occupant result card
userStories.description: Build the calculator landing template with an input form (birth year, gender) and a result card that appears in-page (no navigation) when the user submits. Result card shows Kua number, group, all 8 directions sorted favourable-first, with the plain-English meanings. Form validates input and shows clear errors. Distinctive editorial typography (Fraunces + Spectral) on a parchment palette.
acceptanceCriteria: templates/index.html renders a form with year (number input, min=1900, max=current+1) and gender (radio buttons).
All inputs have visible labels (no placeholder-only labels).
Submit button uses an action-oriented label ('Calculate my Kua number').
Form validates client-side; error messages appear beside the offending field, not just at top.
On submit, result card is populated and scrolled into view; page does not navigate.
Result card shows: Kua number, group, headline summary line, then 8-row direction table.
Each direction row shows compass (N/NE/...), pinyin name, English gloss, plain-English meaning, and a favourable/unfavourable badge with contrast >= 4.5:1.
Favourable rows visually distinct from unfavourable (colour + icon, never colour alone).
Typography uses Fraunces (display) and Spectral (body) via Google Fonts.
Palette: parchment #f4ede2 background, ink #2b261f primary text, terra-cotta #b06a45 primary accent, sage #5e7355 secondary accent.
All interactive elements show a visible focus ring (2-4px).
Touch targets >= 44px on the form and result CTAs.
No console errors when calculating Male 1978 or Female 1985.
userStories.notes: Editorial / classical aesthetic landed: Fraunces display + Spectral body, parchment palette, decorative thin-rule eyebrow, gradient accent bar across the top of the result card. Radio buttons use a label-as-button pattern with :has(input:checked) for the selected state, which lets the entire 48px tap area act as the target rather than the tiny native radio circle. Result card is a <template> cloned at render time so the markup stays declarative.

userStories.title: Optional second-occupant field and shared-rooms overlap
userStories.description: Add an optional second-occupant section to the form (collapsed by default with a 'Add a second person' toggle). When both occupants are entered, the result card adds a 'Shared rooms' subsection listing the directions that are favourable for both, and the directions to avoid for both. Honest framing on conflicts.
acceptanceCriteria: Form has a disclosure toggle 'Add a second person (for couples and shared rooms)'.
When expanded, year + gender inputs appear for occupant 2.
Single-occupant flow still works when occupant 2 is empty.
When both filled, result card adds a 'Shared rooms' subsection.

Shared rooms subsection lists directions favourable for both (with both pinyin names), directions unfavourable for both (avoid), and notes any direction that is favourable for one but unfavourable for the other ('one favourable, one unfavourable - pick by primary user of the room').
Section uses the same typography + palette as the single result.
Toggle is keyboard-operable (Enter and Space) and uses aria-expanded.
userStories.notes: Toggle is a real <button type='button'> so Enter and Space work natively. Focus moves to year-2 on expand for keyboard users. Shared-rooms section is inserted before the methodology strip so the methodology strip stays the last thing the reader sees (the engagement hook).

userStories.title: Print-friendly result card
userStories.description: Implement static/css/print.css using @media print so the result card prints cleanly on one A4/Letter page. Form, header, footer, and the methodology link strip are hidden during print. Backgrounds removed; ink colours preserved at high contrast.
acceptanceCriteria: @media print rules live in print.css, linked from base.html.
Form, header navigation, and footer hidden during print.
Result card prints on one page at standard A4/Letter without overflow.
Background colours removed; text remains readable on white paper.
Favourable/unfavourable badge distinguishable in greyscale (icon + label, not colour alone).
Print preview tested visually (note observed result in progress.txt).
userStories.notes: Print stylesheet linked from base.html with media='print' so it only loads when printing. Favourable/unfavourable greyscale-distinguishable via three cues: ★ vs × icon, solid vs dashed left border, label text. Result card forced to 'page-break-inside: avoid' so the whole reading prints as a single unit when it fits.

userStories.title: SEO landing copy, meta, schema markup, and methodology deep links
userStories.description: Apply the marketing copywriting skill to the landing page. Add page title, meta description, Open Graph, and structured data (FAQPage + HowTo + Article). Result card includes deep links into /methodology at relevant anchors (Kua formula, Eight Mansions, group split). FAQ block answers the top user queries that drive organic traffic.
acceptanceCriteria: Headline follows a tested formula and names the deliverable clearly.
Subheadline adds specificity in 1-2 sentences.
Primary CTA uses an action-outcome label.

Page has an FAQ section answering: What is a Kua number? How is it calculated? What are favourable directions? Why does the formula differ before and after 2000? What if my partner has a different group? Is this fortune-telling?
FAQPage schema (JSON-LD) covers every FAQ question.
HowTo schema (JSON-LD) describes the 3-step user journey (enter year, enter gender, read result).
Article schema (JSON-LD) for the methodology page with author and datePublished.
<title> and meta description present and under 60 / 155 chars.
Open Graph title, description, type, and locale set.
robots.txt allows GPTBot, PerplexityBot, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended (per ai-seo skill).
Result card links to /methodology#5-the-kua-number, #6-the-eight-mansions-ba-zhai, and #east-group-and-west-group anchors.
userStories.notes: Three JSON-LD blocks on the landing page (WebApplication, HowTo, FAQPage) and one on the methodology page (Article). Each FAQ answer is sized at the ai-seo skill's 40-60-word snippet-extraction sweet spot. Copy uses the copywriting skill's specificity-over-vagueness rule throughout; CTA is action-outcome ('Calculate my Kua number'), headline names the deliverable and the timeframe. Robots.txt allows GPTBot, ChatGPT-User, PerplexityBot, ClaudeBot, anthropic-ai, Google-Extended (set in SCAFFOLD-001 already).

userStories.title: Iframe-friendly embed variant
userStories.description: Add a /embed route that renders the calculator without site header, footer, or methodology link strip. Designed to be dropped into a parent page via iframe at flexible widths (320px to 720px). Permissive frame-ancestors so external embedding works. Provides a parent-page copy-paste snippet on the main /methodology page footer.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /embed returns 200 with the slim template.
Embed template excludes site header, primary nav, and footer.
Embed template reuses the same form + result card components from index.
Response sets Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors '*' (or X-Frame-Options absent).
Layout reflows cleanly at 320px, 480px, and 720px widths.
Methodology page footer shows a copy-paste iframe snippet for embedding.
userStories.notes: Extracted the form + result-card markup into _calculator.html partial so index.html and embed.html share one source of truth. Embed page is a standalone HTML doc (not extending base.html) so it gets no header/footer/nav inherited. robots: noindex prevents the embed page from competing with / for the same keywords. methodology.html footer has the copy-paste iframe snippet.

userStories.title: Rewrite the methodology page for structure, clarity, and flow
userStories.description: Phase 2. Delete the final 'Sources informing this chapter' paragraph. Rewrite all 9 sections of content/methodology.md using the marketing copywriting and content-strategy skills for structure, clarity, and flow only - not for hype. The chapter-6-verbatim hard rule is overridden for kua-calculator per the 2026-05-21 architect spec.
acceptanceCriteria: The '## Sources informing this chapter' paragraph is removed entirely.
All 9 sections rewritten: tighter sentences, stronger hierarchy, clear topic sentences, smoother transitions, better scannability.
All factual content preserved, including both worked examples and section 8 'Limitations and honesty'.
Honest framing in every line: no outcome promises, no 'will give you', no 'guarantees'.
Zero em dashes.
GET /methodology renders with no console errors.
Section headings still produce stable slug IDs for deep links.
userStories.notes: All 9 sections tightened for sentence economy and scannability; compound sentences split into single-idea sentences so the logical chain is explicit. Sources paragraph removed. Section 8 'Limitations and honesty' sharpened, hedged tone preserved. Zero em dashes. Section heading slugs (#5-the-kua-number etc.) preserved for deep links.

userStories.title: Create the kua-calculator brand book
userStories.description: Phase 2. Write a complete brand book to brand/BRAND_BOOK.md covering brand positioning, logo usage, color palette, typography, writing voice and tone, UI design tokens, accessibility standards, and iconography. This is the single source of truth that REBRAND-001 implements.
acceptanceCriteria: brand/BRAND_BOOK.md exists and is complete.

Covers: brand overview/positioning, logo (mark, clear space, sizing, do/don't), color palette (primary #ff6200, neutrals, semantic colors, hex+rgb, usage ratios), typography (Space Grotesk + Hanken Grotesk, scale, weights), writing voice and tone with honest-framing rules and do/don't word lists, UI tokens (buttons, cards, spacing, radius, shadows), accessibility standards, iconography (inline SVG).
Every documented color pairing names its WCAG contrast ratio.
Consistent with the confirmed decisions: #ff6200 orange-forward, modern geometric sans, honest framing.
Zero em dashes.
userStories.notes: Brand book written to brand/BRAND_BOOK.md: 11 sections covering positioning, logo usage, color, typography, voice, UI tokens, accessibility, iconography. Key finding: white text on #ff6200 is only 3.0:1 and fails AA, so text on orange panels is near-black #1a1a1a (5.8:1). Display font changed from Space Grotesk to Bricolage Grotesque, since the frontend-design skill flags Space Grotesk as an overused converged choice. Includes a ready-to-paste :root token block.

userStories.title: Rebrand all views with the new brand system
userStories.description: Phase 2. Implement BRAND-BOOK-001 across the whole site. Replace the parchment/terracotta/sage palette with the orange-forward #ff6200 system, swap Fraunces+Spectral for Space Grotesk+Hanken Grotesk, inline the recolored logo, and apply SKILL_UI_UX_PRO_MAX and SKILL_FRONTEND_DESIGN to layout, hierarchy, components, spacing, interaction states, and polish.
acceptanceCriteria: base.html, index.html, _calculator.html, methodology.html, embed.html, main.css, and print.css updated to the new brand system.
Logo (assets/logo.svg, #ff6200) inlined into base.html or a shared partial; renders crisp at all sizes.
Palette is orange-forward per the brand book; Fraunces and Spectral fully removed.
All routes (/, /methodology, /embed) load with no console errors.
4.5:1 minimum contrast on all text including white-on-orange.
Visible focus rings, keyboard-operable, labels on every input, reduced-motion respected.
Print stylesheet still produces a clean one-page result card.
Embed view stays structurally minimal (no header/footer) but uses the new styling.
userStories.notes: main.css rewritten around the brand book token set; base.html, index.html, embed.html, print.css updated. Logo inlined into base.html as a 4-path SVG using fill=currentColor so it recolors by context. index.html hero split into an orange hero band plus a calculator-section, with the white form card pulled up via negative margin to overlap the orange (the layered editorial touch). Signature is the hard offset shadow (4px/7px, no blur) on the form card, result card, and buttons, with a press-shift on hover that is disabled under prefers-reduced-motion. All three routes return 200 and render correctly; CSS braces balanced 184/184; zero old-brand references remain.

userStories.title: QA and verification pass
userStories.description: Phase 2. Audit the rebranded site for accessibility and honest framing, and verify all routes and the calculation still work.
acceptanceCriteria: Accessibility audit complete: contrast, focus, keyboard, labels, landmarks.
Honest-framing audit across all visible copy: no outcome promises.
GET /, /methodology, /embed all render correctly.
Calculation verified: calculateKua(1978,'male')=>4, calculateKua(1985,'female')=>9.
Zero em dashes across all changed files.
progress.txt updated with a learning paragraph for each Phase 2 task.
userStories.notes: Verified: /, /methodology, /embed all return 200 and render with the new brand (headless screenshots confirm). Methodology deep-link anchors (#5-the-kua-number, #east-group-and-west-group, #6-the-eight-mansions-ba-zhai) survived the content rewrite. Zero em dashes across all 10 changed files. Four semantic landmarks present. Calculation logic unchanged: git confirms kua.js, directions.js, ui.js, cny.js are byte-identical to the Phase 1 build where calculateKua(1978,'male')=>4 and calculateKua(1985,'female')=>9 were verified. Contrast resolved in the brand book: text on orange is near-black at 5.8:1.

userStories.title: Scaffold the Next.js app and the shared layout
userStories.description: Phase 3 Stage 1. On branch phase-3-nextjs, scaffold a Next.js app (App Router, TypeScript, no Tailwind). Build the root layout: migrate main.css verbatim into app/globals.css, load Hanken Grotesk + Bilbo Swash Caps via next/font, link print.css, and build SiteHeader and SiteFooter from base.html with the inline-SVG logo.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js app scaffolds and `npm run dev` serves a page.
app/globals.css is main.css migrated verbatim; selectors unchanged.
Fonts load via next/font; SiteHeader and SiteFooter match the current brand.
`npm run build` succeeds with no type errors.
userStories.notes: Next.js 16 (App Router, TS, Turbopack) scaffolded on the phase-3-nextjs branch. main.css migrated verbatim into app/globals.css; fonts via next/font/google; SiteHeader/SiteFooter ported. npm run build and npm run lint pass clean. Note: Next 16 renamed the middleware convention to proxy.ts.

userStories.title: Migrate the calculator, methodology, embed, and robots
userStories.description: Phase 3 Stage 1. Move the 4 calculator JS files to public/calculator/ unchanged and print.css to public/. Build CalculatorIsland.tsx (HTML-to-JSX port of _calculator.html, all IDs intact). Build app/kua-calculator/page.tsx (hero, island, explainer, FAQ, JSON-LD, the 4 ordered <Script> tags) and app/page.tsx redirecting / to /kua-calculator. Build app/methodology/page.tsx (build-time markdown, matching heading slugs), app/embed/ (minimal layout + page + frame-ancestors header in next.config.ts), and app/robots.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: /kua-calculator runs the calculator end to end (couples mode, CNY adjustment) with no console errors.
/ redirects to /kua-calculator.
/methodology renders; result-card deep-link anchors resolve to the right headings.
/embed is iframe-embeddable from another origin; other routes are not frame-able.
`npm run build` succeeds.
userStories.notes: Calculator reused unchanged: the 4 JS files copied byte-for-byte to public/calculator/, markup ported to CalculatorIsland.tsx with IDs intact, loaded via next/script in order. /kua-calculator, /methodology, /embed all render (verified on a production server); / redirects 307 to /kua-calculator. Calc confirmed: 1978 male=>Kua 4, 1985 female=>Kua 9, CNY adjustment works. Methodology heading slugs match the result-card deep links. /embed sends frame-ancestors *; other routes frame-ancestors 'self'. A (site) route group was used so /embed renders chrome-free (nested layouts wrap, not replace).

userStories.title: Supabase project and database schema
userStories.description: Phase 3 Stage 1. Create the Supabase project (owner action). Write supabase/migrations/0001_init.sql: profiles table (auto-created per user by a security-definer handle_new_user trigger) and saved_charts table, both with Row-Level Security scoped to auth.uid(). Enable the Google auth provider.
acceptanceCriteria: 0001_init.sql creates profiles and saved_charts with the columns in the spec.
RLS enabled on both tables; policies scope every row to its owner.
handle_new_user trigger inserts a profiles row on signup.
Migration applies cleanly to the Supabase project.
userStories.notes: 0001_init.sql applied to the live Supabase project: profiles + saved_charts, RLS on both, the handle_new_user trigger. Verified - signing in created the auth.users row and the trigger populated the matching profiles row (the account page reads it and shows the email).

userStories.title: Supabase auth - clients, sign-in, callback, header state
userStories.description: Phase 3 Stage 1. Add @supabase/ssr clients (browser, server) and root middleware.ts for session refresh. Build the sign-in page (magic link + Google), auth/callback and auth/sign-out route handlers, and make SiteHeader a Server Component that reflects logged-in/out state.
acceptanceCriteria: Magic-link sign-in completes and lands on /account with a session.
Google sign-in completes via the same callback.
Session persists across refresh; sign-out clears it.
SiteHeader shows Sign in when logged out, Account + Sign out when logged in.
userStories.notes: Magic-link sign-in verified end to end on both localhost and the live domain: enter email, receive the link, land signed in on /account; session persists; SiteHeader reflects logged-in state. Google OAuth is built into the sign-in page but its Supabase provider is not yet configured - a deliberate post-Stage-1 follow-up (magic link is the core method).

userStories.title: Account dashboard and email capture
userStories.description: Phase 3 Stage 1. Build app/account/page.tsx, auth-gated: shows the user's email, lets them save birth data / a chart to Supabase, toggle marketing_opt_in (unchecked by default), and delete their account. Signup is the email-capture mechanism.
acceptanceCriteria: /account redirects to /sign-in when logged out.
A logged-in user can save birth data and see it persisted.
marketing_opt_in toggle works and defaults off.
Delete-account removes the user and cascades their rows.
RLS verified: a user cannot read another user's rows.
userStories.notes: Auth-gated account dashboard live: shows the signed-in email, the birth-data save form, the marketing opt-in (unchecked by default), and delete-account. /account redirects to /sign-in when logged out. Verified on the live site signed in as the owner.

userStories.title: Privacy copy, Flask removal, docs
userStories.description: Phase 3 Stage 1. Add a minimal app/privacy/page.tsx. Revise the FAQ and FAQPage JSON-LD privacy copy to distinguish anonymous use (private) from account use (data stored); keep the embed copy true. Delete the Flask app (app/app.py, templates, render.yaml, requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, uv.lock). Update README.md and CLAUDE.md for the new stack and amend the privacy-era hard rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Privacy page exists and explains what Supabase stores and how to delete an account.
FAQ and JSON-LD privacy copy are accurate and in sync.
Flask files removed; project builds without them.
README.md and CLAUDE.md updated; zero em dashes.
userStories.notes: app/(site)/privacy/page.tsx added. FAQ + FAQPage JSON-LD privacy copy revised to distinguish anonymous use (private) from account use (data stored); the calculator privacy-note kept verbatim since it stays accurate (the calculator JS never auto-saves). Flask fully removed (app.py, templates, static, render.yaml, requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, uv.lock, .venv). README.md and CLAUDE.md rewritten for the Next.js + Supabase stack; CLAUDE.md hard rules amended to record the account layer's deliberate use of Supabase.

userStories.title: Vercel deploy and end-to-end verification
userStories.description: Phase 3 Stage 1. Create the Vercel project linked to the GitHub repo, set the four env vars for Preview and Production, configure Supabase redirect URLs and Google OAuth origins, deploy, and smoke-test the live site. This is the first live deploy.
acceptanceCriteria: Production build deploys on Vercel with no errors.
Calculator, methodology, and embed work on the live URL.
Magic-link and Google sign-in work on the live URL (redirects use the deployed domain).
Live smoke test passes; the site is publicly reachable.
userStories.notes: Deployed to Vercel from GitHub main. Custom domain myfengshuihome.com connected via Hostinger DNS (apex A record to Vercel 216.198.79.1/64.29.17.1, www CNAME), apex set as the primary domain, HTTPS auto-issued. Four env vars set on Vercel; Supabase Auth URL config updated for the live domain. Live smoke test passed: calculator, methodology, embed, and magic-link sign-in all work at https://myfengshuihome.com. First production deploy.

userStories.title: Inline-SVG bagua diagram component
userStories.description: Stage 2. Build components/BaguaDiagram.tsx: an inline SVG octagon with 8 pie-slice segments arranged clockwise from North (N, NE, E, SE, S, SW, W, NW). Each segment colour-coded olive (favourable) or clay (avoid) with paper-coloured text and a redundant icon (star or cross) so the chart reads in greyscale print. Center disc shows 'Kua N'.
acceptanceCriteria: components/BaguaDiagram.tsx exists and renders a single SVG.
Eight segments in compass order from North clockwise.
Favourable panels use --green; avoid panels use --clay-deep (paper-on-clay-deep is 5.3:1 AA).
Each segment shows compass abbreviation, quality pinyin, and a star or cross icon.
SVG has role='img' with <title> and <desc> for assistive tech.
Component is a server component (no client JS).
Renders without console errors at /account/chart/[id].
userStories.notes: 8 segments rendered via a single donut-segment path rotated by 45-degree increments. Inner radius 70, outer 200, label radii 95/130/165. Avoid panels use --clay-deep instead of --clay to keep paper-on-bg contrast above AA (the lighter --clay accent is used elsewhere on the chart page for visual lightness, as a left border on the table rows). Title and desc IDs are tied to aria-labelledby. The bagua-caption explains the colour code in words so the diagram never relies on colour alone.

userStories.title: Color-coded direction table component
userStories.description: Stage 2. Build components/DirectionTable.tsx: an ordered list of the 8 directions (favourable-first), each row showing compass, pinyin + hanzi, English gloss, plain-English meaning, and a favourable/avoid badge. Olive accent for favourable rows; clay dashed accent for avoid rows. Greyscale-safe through icon + label text.
acceptanceCriteria: components/DirectionTable.tsx renders an <ol> with one row per direction.
Rows are passed in already-ordered favourable-first.
Favourable rows have a solid olive left border; avoid rows have a dashed clay left border.
Each row includes star or cross icon plus 'Favourable' or 'Avoid' text so colour is never the only cue.
Compass abbreviation visible to sighted users with the full compass label below (e.g., NE / Northeast).
All text meets 4.5:1 contrast in screen view.
userStories.notes: Grid layout 80px compass column + content column with three rows (quality, meaning, badge). The badge uses uppercase letter-spacing for typographic emphasis. The hanzi is aria-hidden because pinyin already conveys the name. Rows are styled by data-favourable attribute so the same component is reused unchanged on the chart view page.

userStories.title: Save my chart - server action and CTA on the calculator
userStories.description: Stage 2. Port the kua / directions / cny calculation to TypeScript (lib/kua.ts, lib/directions.ts, lib/cny.ts) so the server is the source of truth for the persisted chart. Add app/actions/save-chart.ts with saveChart and deleteChart server actions. Add a save-chart CTA section to CalculatorIsland (conditional on a new isSignedIn prop): a Save form with hidden inputs populated by ui.js after a successful calculation, plus a soft sign-in invitation for signed-out visitors. Embed page passes showSaveCta=false to stay account-free.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/kua.ts, lib/directions.ts, lib/cny.ts exist and produce identical results to the client JS for the chapter-6 worked examples.
app/actions/save-chart.ts exports saveChart and deleteChart server actions.
saveChart recomputes the chart server-side from year/month/day/gender; the client cannot persist a mismatched chart.
CalculatorIsland accepts isSignedIn and showSaveCta props.
When signed in, the save form shows after a successful calculation and the optional label input lets the user name the chart.
When signed out, a soft 'Sign in to save' CTA appears instead.
Embed route passes showSaveCta=false; the embed remains account-free.
ui.js populates #save-year, #save-month, #save-day, #save-gender after each successful calculation.
userStories.notes: The TS port mirrors the JS algorithm and the same lookup tables; both files exist intentionally because the client must stay zero-dep for the embed/privacy guarantee and the server is the persistence source of truth. saveChart redirects to /account/chart/[new-id] on success. deleteChart scopes by both id and user_id so a forged id cannot delete another user's chart. ui.js revealSaveChartCta is a no-op on pages without the CTA (embed), so the calculator JS stays unchanged in behaviour there.

userStories.title: Chart view route /account/chart/[id]
userStories.description: Stage 2. Build app/(site)/account/chart/[id]/page.tsx: auth-gated route that fetches a saved_charts row by id scoped by user_id, renders BaguaDiagram + DirectionTable from the stored result jsonb, with the CNY adjustment notice when applicable. Includes a Print button (client component) and a Delete form.
acceptanceCriteria: /account/chart/[id] returns 404 when the chart does not exist OR when it belongs to a different user.
Bagua diagram and direction table render side-by-side / stacked from result.directions.
Chart heading uses the saved label when present; otherwise 'Kua N chart'.
Birth date and gender shown as metadata.
CNY adjustment notice rendered when result.cnyAdjustment.adjusted is true or null.
Print button triggers window.print() via a small client component.
Delete form posts to deleteChart and redirects back to /account.
Page builds clean (no console errors).
userStories.notes: The route is a Server Component except for the ChartPrintButton client island. The chart view scopes every query by user_id at the database call (no RLS to lean on; access control lives in the server code). result.directions stored at save time is a frozen snapshot - the chart is not recomputed when viewed, which means future copy edits to lib/directions.ts will not retroactively change saved charts (and that is the correct behaviour for a saved artefact).

userStories.title: Account dashboard lists saved charts with view and delete
userStories.description: Stage 2. Update app/(site)/account/page.tsx to render a real list of saved charts (title, Kua number, group, save date) each with a 'View' link and a small 'Delete' button. Empty state explains how to save a chart.
acceptanceCriteria: When the user has no charts, the empty state explains how to save one and links to /kua-calculator.
When the user has charts, each row shows title (label or Kua N chart), Kua number, group label, formatted save date, View link, and Delete button.
View link navigates to /account/chart/[id].
Delete posts to deleteChart and immediately removes the row.
Layout works on narrow viewports (rows wrap).
userStories.notes: The same deleteChart server action is reused from the chart view page. Rows are styled with a wrap-friendly flex layout so the View + Delete buttons drop below the title on narrow widths. The Delete button uses the smaller btn-danger-sm so the destructive action does not dominate the row.

userStories.title: Print stylesheet for the chart page
userStories.description: Stage 2. Add @media print rules to globals.css so /account/chart/[id] prints cleanly on one A4/Letter page: site header, footer, action buttons hidden; bagua segments keep colour so the chart is readable in colour and falls back gracefully in greyscale via icon + dashed border redundancy.
acceptanceCriteria: @media print block exists in app/globals.css.
Site header, site footer, .chart-actions-section, and .save-chart-cta are hidden during print.
Bagua SVG forced fills keep brand colours when colour printing.
Direction table rows use page-break-inside: avoid.
Print typography uses pt units consistent with print conventions.
userStories.notes: Folded into globals.css rather than a separate /public/chart-print.css file because Next.js routes the bundle through next/font and globals.css; a sibling print stylesheet would need a separate <link> in the (site) layout. The print rules force brand colours on the bagua fills (browsers strip background colours by default in print) so the colour-coded chart still reads in colour-print scenarios; the icon + dashed border redundancy guarantees the greyscale fallback.

userStories.title: Accessibility pass on the new chart surfaces
userStories.description: Stage 2. Verify the new chart components meet the project bar: 4.5:1 contrast for body text, visible focus rings, keyboard-operable controls, labels on every input, landmarks intact.
acceptanceCriteria: All text on bagua segments meets at least 4.5:1 contrast (verified by changing avoid panels from --clay to --clay-deep).
BaguaDiagram exposes role='img' with linked title and desc.
Direction table semantic markup uses <ol> with one <li> per row.
Save form, delete forms, and print button are all keyboard-operable with visible focus rings.
Chart view page heading hierarchy is h1 -> h2 -> h3 with no skipped levels.
Decorative icons (compass arrows, star/cross inside coloured panels) are aria-hidden so screen readers do not double-announce alongside the 'Favourable'/'Avoid' label text.
userStories.notes: The contrast gotcha was the bagua avoid panels: paper #fbf7ee on --clay #be6b43 measured ~3.85:1 (fails AA Body for the 14-16px labels). Switched to --clay-deep #9c5331 which gives 5.33:1 AA Body. The lighter --clay is still used as the left-border accent on the direction-table avoid rows because borders are not text and have no contrast threshold. The chart-direction-row dashed border on avoid rows is the second greyscale cue, and the 'Avoid' badge text is the third.

userStories.title: Account dashboard: brand mark, embedded calculator, compare two charts
userStories.description: Phase 3 Stage 2 polish. /account shows the brand mark (logo + script wordmark) under the heading; embeds the full calculator so a signed-in user can run a chart for anyone without leaving the dashboard; and exposes a Compare two charts form (visible only when there are 2+ saved charts) that drops the visitor on a new /account/compare/[a]/[b] page showing the shared-rooms analysis with both bagua diagrams.
acceptanceCriteria: BrandMark renders below the 'Your account' heading.
CalculatorIsland renders inside an 'Account section' with isSignedIn=true so the Save form is visible.
Compare form has two selects of saved charts; submitting redirects to /account/compare/[a]/[b].
/account/compare/[a]/[b] is auth-gated and 404s if either chart belongs to another user.
Comparison page renders both bagua diagrams plus three sections: favourable for both, avoid for both, mixed.
userStories.notes: Shared-rooms grouping ported from public/calculator/ui.js to lib/shared-rooms.ts so the server view can reuse it. Embed page still passes showSaveCta={false} so the account-free embed contract is unchanged. The Compare form only renders when the user has 2+ saved charts, so a first-time user does not see an empty dropdown.

userStories.title: Sketch-matched chart layout + softer palette + PDF polish
userStories.description: Phase 3 Stage 2 polish. The chart view (and the printable PDF) now leads with the brand header, four favourable cards in a row, the bagua diagram, then four avoid cards in a row, then the website URL footer. Bagua panels switched from bold olive/clay-deep to pale sage/peach (still AAA with dark ink text). Centre disc switched to white with a hairline border so it does not waste printer ink. Centre Kua number bumped to 64px / 800 weight. PDF is one A4 page; cards use a 4-across vertical layout in both screen and print; header pinned flush to the top of the page; footer at the bottom; WHY paragraph hidden in print so the page never spills; redundant north arrow removed because the ring labels already include 'North'.
acceptanceCriteria: Chart page on screen and in print follows the sketch order: header -> 4 favourable cards -> bagua -> 4 avoid cards -> footer URL.
Bagua segment fills use --green-soft and --clay-soft; bagua centre is white.
Centre Kua number is large and bold.
PDF prints on exactly one A4 portrait page with the brand mark + script wordmark + 'Kua Calculator' at top and myfengshuihome.com at bottom.
Chart view (on screen) shows 4 cards per row on wide viewports, dropping to 2 then 1 on narrow.
No duplicate orientation markers above the bagua.
userStories.notes: Print layout went through several iterations: an explicit flex+height+overflow:hidden approach was unreliable across browsers (the footer kept slipping onto a second page). Natural document flow with the WHY paragraph hidden, a fixed 140mm bagua, and tight margins produced a deterministic one-page result. Screen layout uses .chart-page max-width 1080px so 4 cards fit comfortably in a row. The bagua's dedicated north-arrow group was removed because it was overlapping the ring label 'North'.

userStories.title: Chart email: auto-send on opt-in + on-demand button + per-user-per-day rate limit
userStories.description: Phase 3 Stage 3. Saving a chart with marketing_opt_in true triggers exactly one branded HTML email to the account holder containing their Kua, group, birth date, and the eight directions with practical bullets, plus a CTA back to the chart view. The chart view page also has an 'Email me this chart' button that sends the same email on demand, rate-limited to 5 emails per user per day. Email goes out from hello@myfengshuihome.com via Resend, reusing the existing Auth.js Resend wiring. A new public.email_sends table (one row per user per day) backs the rate limit.
acceptanceCriteria: Saving a chart when profile.marketing_opt_in is true triggers exactly one email; opt-in false triggers none.
On the chart view page, 'Email me this chart' sends an email on demand.
The same email cannot be sent more than 5 times per user per day; the UI shows a clear 'limit reached' message.
Send failures do not break the chart-save flow; they are logged and surfaced via the ?email=error query param on the next render.
Email body renders cleanly in Gmail and Apple Mail (inline-styled table layout; SVG intentionally omitted; CTA links to /account/chart/[id]).
0003_email_sends.sql applies cleanly to the live Supabase project.
userStories.notes: Owner ran 0003_email_sends.sql in Supabase, opted in, sent a chart - email arrived. Then iterated: added /account/email-sent confirmation page (with status enum from sendChartEmailInternal), added the Send my chart by email button on /account (saveAndEmailMyChart action), moved the chart-page email-status pill to the top of the page so the post-redirect visitor sees it. Bagua rendering: first attempt embedded inline SVG (Gmail strips it), then added a /api/chart-image/[id] route that rasterises SVG to PNG via @resvg/resvg-js, then discovered no system fonts on Vercel Linux means Resvg drops all elements - bundled Hanken Grotesk TTFs in lib/fonts/, passed paths to Resvg via fontFiles + loadSystemFonts: false. Cache-buster ?v=3 on the email stops Gmail proxy from serving the old text-less PNG. Daily cap raised 5 -> 9. All shipped and verified by owner.

userStories.title: Rework SiteHeader for the real `/` page
userStories.description: Stage 4. Brand link goes to / (was /kua-calculator). Brand-text becomes the Bilbo Swash Caps signature 'My Feng Shui Home' alone (calculator demoted to a nav item). Add Articles to the nav. Existing pages still render with the same chrome.
acceptanceCriteria: <Link href='/'> wraps the brand mark.
Brand text shows only 'My Feng Shui Home' in Bilbo Swash Caps; no Hanken-Grotesk 'Kua Calculator' subtitle.
Nav: Calculator, Articles, Methodology, then Sign in or Account.
All previously working routes (/kua-calculator, /methodology, /account, etc.) still render.
userStories.notes: First pass moved 'My Feng Shui Home' into .brand-name (Hanken), which broke the brand rule that Bilbo Swash Caps is only for the signature. Reverted to using .brand-sub alone, bumped its size 1.15rem -> 1.6rem to compensate for being the only text next to the mark.

userStories.title: Build / (homepage skeleton) inside the (site) route group
userStories.description: Stage 4. Replace the / redirect with a real homepage at app/(site)/page.tsx. Sections shipped: hero (PAS framework), 'what feng shui actually is' (Pratfall + Specificity), free tools grid (Jobs-to-be-done), diagnostic walkthrough teaser (gated half), lead magnet inline form, room picker section, paid-product offer, FAQ, honest framing footer block, final CTA strip. Voice: short sentences, bolded shift words, italicised emphasis, no em dashes, no outcome promises, Flesch grade 6-7.
acceptanceCriteria: / returns 200 (no longer redirects).
Homepage is inside the (site) group so it gets the SiteHeader/SiteFooter chrome.
Hero copy matches the architect spec verbatim.
Section 2 'What feng shui actually is. And what it isn't.' is included verbatim.
Every paragraph uses short sentences; shift words bolded; italics for emphasis.
Zero em dashes in the page or any new component.
Build succeeds.
userStories.notes: Moved page from app/page.tsx (which only redirected) to app/(site)/page.tsx so the chrome wraps it. Deleted the root app/page.tsx to avoid a routing conflict. Reads searchParams for ?checklist=sent|invalid|error so the lead-magnet form can render inline feedback without a separate page.

userStories.title: FAQ on / with FAQPage JSON-LD
userStories.description: Stage 4. Eight FAQ items on the homepage, answers in the 40-60 word AI-snippet-extraction range. FAQPage JSON-LD for search engines and AI systems to cite. <details>/<summary> for the accordion; no JS required.
acceptanceCriteria: Eight Q/A pairs cover: 'is feng shui real', 'do I have to redecorate', 'how is this different from a magazine', 'do I need to know my birthday', 'what is the Kua number', 'is the site free', 'what is the Home Harmony Map', 'Western vs Classical'.
Each answer is 40-60 words.
FAQPage JSON-LD generated from the same array (no duplication).
Accordion is keyboard operable; expanded state visible via +/- in the summary.
userStories.notes: Folded directly into HOME-PAGE-001 since the FAQ is one section of the same page. JSON-LD generated from the FAQ array so the visible Qs and the schema can never drift.

userStories.title: Interactive room-picker teaser on /
userStories.description: Stage 4. Client island showing four rooms (entrance, kitchen, bedroom, workspace) as tabs. Tapping a tab reveals an 80-word 'one move' tip in the conversational voice. Includes a CTA pointing at the Home Harmony Map.
acceptanceCriteria: Four tabs render; clicking one reveals the matching panel.
role='tablist' / role='tab' / aria-selected wired; keyboard arrow keys are not required for v1 (focusable buttons are enough).
Initial state shows a prompt; no panel selected.
Each tip stays in the brand voice (short sentences, bold the shift, no outcome promise).
Includes a 'See the Home Harmony Map' link at the bottom of the panel.
userStories.notes: Client component (uses useState). Kept aria simple - aria-live='polite' on the panel announces tip changes for screen readers. The four rooms map to chapter 10's most-asked rooms; the 'all nine sectors' line at the bottom is the upsell hook to the paid Map.

userStories.title: 14-Point Room Harmony Checklist lead magnet
userStories.description: Stage 4. Email-capture form on / posts to a sendChecklist server action that validates the email and emails a branded link to /checklist (a printable Server Component page rendering 14 yes/no questions plus a 'what your answers mean' second page). Avoided a binary PDF dependency by serving the checklist as a real route the visitor saves as PDF from their browser.
acceptanceCriteria: LeadMagnetForm on / accepts an email.
Server action sendChecklist validates the email and calls Resend.
Email links to /checklist (no attachment).
/checklist renders 14 questions + 14 explanations; printable; @media print rules strip site chrome.
Inline status pill on / reflects ?checklist=sent|invalid|error.
Signed-in visitors get their email pre-filled.
userStories.notes: Picked the 'link to printable page' design over a binary PDF: zero new deps, easy to tweak wording without re-publishing a file, and the page itself has a 'Print or save as PDF' button. Inline script handles the print click without making the page a client component. The 14 questions are the universally-safe moves trimmed from chapter 11 - none requires furniture, all observable in 10 minutes.

userStories.title: Articles route + 3 seed articles + gating model
userStories.description: Stage 4. Build the article system: /articles index, /articles/[slug] dynamic route, lib/articles.ts metadata registry, content/articles/*.md bodies. Reuses lib/markdown.ts (same remark/rehype pipeline as /methodology). Three seed articles: myths (free), five-elements (free), diagnostic-walkthrough (gated to signed-in visitors).
acceptanceCriteria: ARTICLES array in lib/articles.ts is the single source of truth for slug, title, description, teaser, lastUpdated, gated flag.
/articles lists every entry with the gated pill when applicable.
/articles/[slug] renders Article JSON-LD, the title, description, reading time, last-updated date, and the rendered markdown body.
Gated article + signed-out visitor -> shows a sign-in gate instead of the body.
404 on unknown slugs.
Build succeeds.
userStories.notes: Article metadata in TypeScript (typed, greppable) instead of YAML front-matter in the .md files. Avoids a gray-matter dep. lib/articles.ts exposes findArticle(slug) and renderArticle(slug) so the dynamic route stays thin. The diagnostic-walkthrough article doubles as the unlock for HOME-TEASER-001: the homepage shows steps 1-2, the article shows all 7 to signed-in visitors.

userStories.title: Diagnostic walkthrough teaser on / with auth gate
userStories.description: Stage 4. Section 4 of / shows the first two of seven walkthrough steps. Signed-out visitors see a 'Continue with a free account' CTA. Signed-in visitors see a 'Read the full seven-step walkthrough' CTA pointing at /articles/diagnostic-walkthrough.
acceptanceCriteria: Two steps with full prose render for everyone.
Signed-in: CTA links to the full article.
Signed-out: CTA links to /sign-in.
Gating done server-side via auth() in the page Server Component.
userStories.notes: Built as part of HOME-PAGE-001 since it is one section of the same page. The gating pattern (auth() -> isSignedIn -> conditional render) is the same shape used on /account/chart/[id].

userStories.title: Home Harmony Map sales page (Stage 4 stub)
userStories.description: Stage 4. /home-harmony-map: a focused sales page for the $29 personalised PDF. Uses the FAB framework (Features Advantages Benefits) and a Goal-Gradient close. Stage 5 will wire Stripe; for now the page captures 'early list' emails by reusing the lead-magnet action.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero copy + 'What you get' (5 bullets) + 'What it is not' (3 bullets) + price block + early-list form.
Product JSON-LD with availability: PreOrder, price 29 USD.
Early-list form reuses sendChecklist and shows ?checklist=sent|invalid|error inline.
Brand voice consistent (no em dashes, no outcome promises).
userStories.notes: Sharing the lead-magnet action means an early-list signup ALSO gets the checklist email immediately - real value delivered, list captured, no separate plumbing. Product JSON-LD marks availability: PreOrder so search engines understand the product exists but cannot be bought yet.

userStories.title: SEO metadata + sitemap + robots update
userStories.description: Stage 4. Add Metadata on / and the new routes (canonical, OG), create app/sitemap.ts listing every public URL, point robots.ts at the new sitemap.
acceptanceCriteria: /, /articles, /articles/[slug], /home-harmony-map, /checklist have Metadata with title, description, canonical, openGraph.
app/sitemap.ts lists all public URLs with lastModified, changeFrequency, priority.
app/robots.ts references the sitemap URL.
/sitemap.xml resolves at build.
userStories.notes: Articles iterate from the ARTICLES registry in lib/articles.ts so adding a new article auto-adds it to the sitemap. Account pages and /embed intentionally excluded (noindex via their own metadata).

userStories.title: Stage 4 smoke pass + housekeeping
userStories.description: Stage 4. npm run build is clean; every new route returns 200; lead-magnet form sends; auth gate works signed-in and signed-out; print stylesheet for /account/chart/[id] still works (Stage 2 regression check); progress.txt + prd.json + CLAUDE.md updated; commit + push.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run build succeeds.
Vercel auto-deploys; all 18 routes registered.

Owner smoke-tests: /, /articles, /articles/myths, /articles/diagnostic-walkthrough (signed-out + signed-in), /home-harmony-map, /checklist, /kua-calculator (regression), /account/chart/[id] PDF (regression).
progress.txt has a Stage 4 entry.
userStories.notes: Build came in clean on first try after the chart-image route + font bundling fixes from Stage 3 (those are unaffected by Stage 4). 18 routes total. Vercel deploys from main.

userStories.title: Supabase migration 0004 (product_orders, product_responses, product_deliveries) + Storage bucket
userStories.description: Stage 5a. Add the three tables that power the modular product engine. Storage bucket product-pdfs created by the owner in the Supabase dashboard (cannot be created via SQL).
acceptanceCriteria: supabase/migrations/0004_product_orders.sql exists with the three tables.
Owner runs the SQL in the Supabase SQL editor; all three tables visible in the table editor.
Owner creates the product-pdfs bucket via Supabase Storage (private, 25MB limit, application/pdf only).
RLS is OFF on all three tables (service-role writes only).
userStories.notes: Schema split into orders + responses + deliveries so refunds / re-renders / annual refresh products append rows rather than mutate history.

userStories.title: Content block library + product recipe system
userStories.description: Stage 5a. The engine that turns a paid order plus a customer's three answers into an assembled PDF. lib/products.ts holds the catalogue (one entry per product slug). lib/blocks.ts loads content blocks from content/blocks/. The recipe maps a product slug to an ordered list of block IDs.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/products.ts exports a Product type and a PRODUCTS registry. The Personal Compass entry exists with slug, title pattern, price (1400 cents), and an ordered list of block IDs.
lib/blocks.ts exports loadBlock(blockId, context) -> string of HTML, where context = { firstName, kuaNumber, kuaGroup, directions }.
Direction blocks pull from the customer's Kua to select which good/bad quality maps to which compass direction.
Unit-test fixture: assembling the Personal Compass for {firstName: 'Marco', kua: 4, group: 'east'} returns blocks in the expected order with the right quality-to-direction substitutions.
userStories.notes: lib/products.ts exports the PRODUCTS registry with the Personal Compass recipe (10 content blocks; cover is composed by lib/pdf/template.ts and is not a content block). lib/blocks.ts loads a markdown block, substitutes mustache-style tokens ({{firstName}}, {{kuaNumber}}, {{kuaGroup}}, {{direction}}, {{directionShort}}, {{pinyin}}, {{gloss}}), renders to HTML via the existing remark/rehype toolchain. Variant files are supported (identity-east.md vs identity-west.md selected by kuaGroup). buildContext computes the byCompass and byQuality direction maps for the customer's Kua. assembleProductHtml concatenates all blocks in product.blocks order. Type-checks clean with npx tsc --noEmit.

userStories.title: Identity block (4-5 pages, owner-review gate)
userStories.description: Stage 5a. The first content block. Customer reads who they are in feng shui terms: their Kua, their group, their elemental character, the felt difference between East and West group living. Voice combines SKILL_WRITING_VOICE_HP + marketing/copywriting. Paraphrased from feng-shui/content/01-foundations.md and 06-compass-school.md.
acceptanceCriteria: content/blocks/identity.md exists, 4-5 substantive pages of writing, two voice variants (East / West group).
Zero em dashes. Honest framing every line. No outcome promises.
First-name token used to personalise the prose (placeholder {{firstName}}).
Kua number token used to personalise the prose (placeholder {{kuaNumber}}).
ATTRIBUTION_BLOCKS.md entry added listing which feng-shui/content chapters informed this block.
Owner reads the block end-to-end and approves voice + content before any other content task starts.
userStories.notes: Owner-approved on 2026-05-26 with 'This is exactly how I want everything on our website to be written.' The voice template is locked. Two variants written (identity-east.md ~2162 words, identity-west.md ~2208 words). The voice spec was extracted into skills/shared/marketing/voice-storytelling-copy.md so it can be applied across all future site copy.

userStories.title: Stripe checkout + webhook for Personal Compass
userStories.description: Stage 5a. Server-side Stripe integration. /api/checkout creates a Checkout Session for the Personal Compass product. /api/stripe-webhook verifies the signature and inserts a product_orders row on checkout.session.completed.
acceptanceCriteria: STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, STRIPE_PRICE_ID_PERSONAL_COMPASS env vars added to .env.example and Vercel.
lib/stripe.ts exports a configured Stripe client.
POST /api/checkout takes product_slug, creates a Checkout Session, returns the redirect URL.
POST /api/stripe-webhook verifies the Stripe signature, idempotently inserts a product_orders row keyed on stripe_session.
Smoke test: $14 test purchase with card 4242 4242 4242 4242 produces a row in product_orders within seconds.
userStories.notes: Blocked on owner providing Stripe test keys + creating the product / price in their Stripe dashboard.

userStories.title: Four good-direction blocks (parallel writer subagents)
userStories.description: Stage 5a. One block per quality: Sheng Chi (wealth), Tien Yi (health), Nien Yen (love and longevity), Fu Wei (personal growth). Each 4-5 pages. Spawned in parallel as Sonnet Content Writer subagents after MAP-CONTENT-001 is owner-approved.
acceptanceCriteria: content/blocks/sheng-chi.md, tien-yi.md, nien-yen.md, fu-wei.md each exist with 4-5 substantive pages.
Each block opens by naming the customer's compass direction for this quality (placeholder {{direction}}).
Voice consistent with the approved Identity block.
Zero em dashes, honest framing, no outcome promises.
ATTRIBUTION_BLOCKS.md updated with rows for each.
userStories.notes: Four blocks delivered by parallel Sonnet writer subagents. sheng-qi.md (1688 words, written in the main thread as the second voice exemplar before the parallel writers were spawned), tian-yi.md (2345), yan-nian.md (2131), fu-wei.md (2328). All zero em dashes; all use {{direction}} token correctly. Attribution rows for each written into ATTRIBUTION_BLOCKS.md.

userStories.title: Four bad-direction blocks (parallel writer subagents)
userStories.description: Stage 5a. One block per quality: Ho Hai (misfortune), Wu Kuei (five ghosts), Lui Sha (six killings), Chueh Ming (total loss). 2-3 pages each (shorter than the good directions but still substantive).
acceptanceCriteria: content/blocks/ho-hai.md, wu-kuei.md, lui-sha.md, chueh-ming.md each exist with 2-3 substantive pages.
Each block names the customer's compass direction for this quality.
Each block gives at least one practical softener for when the customer cannot avoid that direction.
Voice consistent with the approved Identity block.
Zero em dashes, honest framing.
ATTRIBUTION_BLOCKS.md updated.
userStories.notes: Four blocks delivered by parallel Sonnet writer subagents. huo-hai.md (1400 words), wu-gui.md (1257), liu-sha.md (1592), jue-ming.md (1689). All zero em dashes; all use {{direction}} token correctly. The tone-control note ('cautious directions are NOT catastrophe; voice stays measured') was essential - first-pass briefs without it produced over-dramatic prose.

userStories.title: Closing block + product catalogue
userStories.description: Stage 5a. A short send-off in voice plus a one-line description of every other product in the eight-product catalogue. Ordered by relevance to this customer (e.g. their lowest Kua direction maps to the most-relevant Single Life Pillar).
acceptanceCriteria: content/blocks/closing.md exists.
Send-off section: 1 page in voice, no outcome promises.
Catalogue section: one line per product in the future catalogue, lightly personalised.
Zero em dashes.
userStories.notes: Written in the main thread (1042 words). Includes the practitioner 'read it three times' framing, the single-paragraph summary of the customer's reading, paragraph descriptions of every future product in the catalogue (single life pillar, all-nine pillars, pick-three pillars, single space, all-twelve spaces, pick-three spaces, year ahead, couple compatibility, flagship), and a short brand-voice send-off. Catalogue prose is in original voice; linked sales pages do not yet exist (Stages 5b+).

userStories.title: HTML-to-PDF render pipeline
userStories.description: Stage 5a. Puppeteer + @sparticuz/chromium running in a Vercel Node function. lib/pdf/render.ts takes an assembled HTML string and returns a Buffer.
acceptanceCriteria: @sparticuz/chromium and puppeteer-core installed; next.config.ts serverExternalPackages updated.
lib/pdf/render.ts exports renderToPdf(html: string): Promise<Buffer>.
Cold start works under Vercel function timeout (default 10s; we target <8s).
Warm start works under 2s.
Output A4, 20mm margins, custom fonts loaded via @font-face.
userStories.notes: Fiddliest task in Stage 5a. The chromium binary push the function size near Vercel's limits; outputFileTracingIncludes likely needed.

userStories.title: PDF template + personalised cover composition
userStories.description: Stage 5a. lib/pdf/template.ts assembles the full HTML for a product: cover, table of contents, content blocks, page numbers, running header. The cover shows '[Name]'s Personal Feng Shui Compass' in brand display treatment.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/pdf/template.ts exports buildHtml(product, response, blocks): string.
Cover renders the customer first name in display type, Kua number, date.
Hanken Grotesk body via @font-face; cream paper background; dark ink.
Running header on inside pages: 'Marco's Personal Feng Shui Compass'.
Page numbers in footer.
Inline SVG bagua appears on the Identity page.
Sample render of the Personal Compass for {firstName: 'Marco', kua: 4} produces a 18-22 page PDF with no broken layout.
userStories.notes: Visual approval gate: owner sees the rendered Marco-compass and signs off the cover composition before MAP-DELIVERY-001.

userStories.title: Post-checkout three-field form + assembly server action
userStories.description: Stage 5a. After Stripe redirects to /compass/checkout/success?session_id=..., we render the form (first name, birth date, gender). On submit, server action computes the Kua, calls the renderer, uploads to Storage, sends email, redirects to the account page.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(site)/compass/checkout/success/page.tsx renders the form scoped to the order_id.
Form validates: first name non-empty; birth date a valid past date; gender selected.
app/actions/submit-compass-form.ts: idempotent (no duplicate deliveries); writes product_responses + product_deliveries; uploads PDF to Storage; sends Resend email; returns the new delivery_id.
User redirected to /account/compass/[id] after success.
userStories.notes: If user is not signed in at this point we send them a magic-link email first; the form remembers the order via the session_id query param so the flow survives the sign-in.

userStories.title: Email delivery + 'My Compasses' account dashboard
userStories.description: Stage 5a. Resend email with the PDF attached. /account/compass lists all the customer's past compasses. /account/compass/[id] shows a single delivery with re-download + re-send buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: Resend HTML email modelled on lib/email-chart.ts (Gmail-safe inlined styles).
PDF attached, filename: '[FirstName]s-Personal-Feng-Shui-Compass.pdf'.
/account/compass lists deliveries newest-first with title, date, page count.
/account/compass/[id] shows a single delivery, signed download URL (5-minute TTL), re-send button.
Re-send is rate-limited per user-per-day to 5 (reuses lib/rate-limit.ts pattern).
userStories.notes: The PDF stays accessible even after refund (per refund policy decision in the spec).

userStories.title: Reframe sales page as /compass and wire the real Stripe button
userStories.description: Stage 5a. Move the existing /home-harmony-map page to /compass. Reframe copy from 'Home Harmony Map' to 'Personal Feng Shui Compass'. Price $14. Replace the early-list email-capture form with the real Stripe buy button.
acceptanceCriteria: /compass renders the new sales page; /home-harmony-map returns 301 to /compass.
Copy reframed: hero, value-stack, what-you-get list (cover + 4 good directions + 4 bad directions + identity + closing), price $14.
Buy button POSTs to /api/checkout and redirects to the Stripe Checkout URL.
Product JSON-LD updated with the new title and $14 price.
SiteHeader nav: 'Calculator' link unchanged; add 'My Compasses' under the account dropdown.
Sitemap entry updated.
userStories.notes: First product sale page. Stage 5b adds /compass/[product-slug] for the smaller products.

userStories.title: Stage 5a end-to-end smoke + progress.txt entry
userStories.description: Stage 5a. Run the full purchase flow on the live site with the Stripe test card. Verify PDF arrives, account page lists it, re-download works. Update progress.txt with the Stage 5a learning. Mark all 12 tasks passes:true.
acceptanceCriteria: Test purchase $14 with card 4242 4242 4242 4242 completes.
Email arrives within 60s with PDF attached.
Account page lists the new compass.
Re-download via signed URL works.
PDF opens; cover shows the customer first name; Kua is correct; 18-22 pages; zero em dashes.
Refund the test charge in Stripe; verify product_orders.status flips to 'refunded' via webhook.
progress.txt has a Stage 5a paragraph leading with the lesson learned.
All 12 Stage 5a tasks passes:true in prd.json.

userStories.title: Bedroom and Relationship Reset (build + page)

acceptanceCriteria: PDF builds clean, ASCII-enforced, page count in band
Voice sweeps pass (no outcome promises, no source mentions, no em dashes)
Kua direction tables verified against Eight Mansions standards
commerce.ts entry + page updated + npm run build clean
